description: Overhaul the local development experience: add directory-based plugin discovery (localPluginDirs), fix the reload storm with coalescing debounce and batch suppression, move checkForUpdates out of the reload hot path, write portable ~/paths in auto-registration, add unknown config key warnings with Levenshtein suggestions, and add --filter/--affected flags for plugin builds. Each feature includes comprehensive E2E test coverage.

userStories.title: Config schema v2→v3: add localPluginDirs field + portable path normalization
userStories.description: Add a new localPluginDirs field to OpentabsConfig and create the v2→v3 config migration that introduces the field and normalizes existing absolute paths under HOME to ~/ prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: OpentabsConfig interface includes localPluginDirs?: string[]
parseConfigRecord parses record.localPluginDirs the same way it parses localPlugins (array of strings, filter non-strings)
New configs created by loadConfig include localPluginDirs: [] in the default object
CURRENT_CONFIG_VERSION is 3 in config-migrations.ts
v2→v3 migration normalizes localPlugins entries: absolute paths starting with os.homedir() are converted to ~/... prefix
v2→v3 migration does NOT convert already-relative paths (starting with ./ or ../)
v2→v3 migration does NOT convert paths not under HOME (e.g., /tmp/...)
v2→v3 migration is idempotent: ~/... paths stay as ~/ on re-migration
v2→v3 migration does NOT auto-consolidate localPlugins into localPluginDirs — users opt in manually
ServerState includes localPluginDirs: string[] field
Existing config-migrations unit tests still pass
New unit tests cover the v2→v3 migration edge cases

userStories.title: scanLocalPluginDir + discovery pipeline integration
userStories.description: Add scanLocalPluginDir function to resolver.ts and integrate localPluginDirs expansion into the reload pipeline with path-level deduplication before calling discoverPlugins.
acceptanceCriteria: scanLocalPluginDir(dir) returns absolute paths of immediate child directories containing package.json with opentabs field
scanLocalPluginDir skips children without package.json or without opentabs field
scanLocalPluginDir returns empty array for non-existent directory (logs warning)
scanLocalPluginDir does NOT recurse into subdirectories of subdirectories
reloadCore expands localPluginDirs into individual paths between loadConfig and discoverPlugins
Path-level deduplication resolves symlinks via realpath before comparing
localPlugins entries take precedence over localPluginDirs-scanned entries (appear first in deduped list)
state.localPluginDirs is populated from config during reload

userStories.title: Server-side reload coalescing on POST /reload
userStories.description: Replace the rate limiter on POST /reload with a 500ms coalescing debounce. Multiple callers within the window share one reload. Auth is checked per-request before coalescing.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /reload no longer returns 429 Too Many Requests
Concurrent POST /reload requests within 500ms are coalesced into a single performConfigReload call
The debounce timer resets on each new request (waits 500ms after the LAST request)
All coalesced callers receive the same JSON response with ok, plugins, durationMs
Auth (checkBearerAuth) is checked per-request BEFORE entering the coalesce pool — invalid auth returns 401 immediately
Coalescing state is stored on ServerState (survives hot reload)
Rate limiters on other endpoints (/extension/reload, /plugin-settings, etc.) are unchanged

userStories.title: Client-side batch reload suppression
userStories.description: Add OPENTABS_SKIP_NOTIFY env var to notifyServer in build.ts. Update scripts/plugins.ts to set it during batch builds and fire a single POST /reload at the end.
acceptanceCriteria: notifyServer() in build.ts returns early when OPENTABS_SKIP_NOTIFY=1 is set
scripts/plugins.ts passes OPENTABS_SKIP_NOTIFY=1 in the spawn environment for each plugin build
scripts/plugins.ts sends a single POST /reload after all plugins are built
The single POST /reload reads port from ~/.opentabs/server.port and secret from ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json
When the server is not running, the final reload fails silently (build still succeeds)

userStories.title: Move checkForUpdates out of reload path
userStories.description: Remove checkForUpdates calls from both performReload and performConfigReload. Run version check once at startup (fire-and-forget) and on a 6-hour periodic timer stored on ServerState.
acceptanceCriteria: performReload does NOT call checkForUpdates
performConfigReload does NOT call checkForUpdates
The plugins.changed notification after checkForUpdates is removed from both functions
A new runVersionCheck function is exported from reload.ts
runVersionCheck is called fire-and-forget after initial performReload in index.ts
A 6-hour periodic timer calls runVersionCheck, stored as state.versionCheckTimerId
The timer is cleared in installShutdownHandlers
The timer is stored on ServerState (not module-level) to survive hot reload correctly
Existing unit test at reload.test.ts that asserts checkForUpdates during performConfigReload is updated

userStories.title: Portable paths in registerInConfig
userStories.description: Update registerInConfig in build.ts to write ~/  prefix for plugin paths under HOME instead of absolute paths.
acceptanceCriteria: toPortablePath function converts absolute paths starting with homedir() to ~/... prefix
toPortablePath returns absolute paths unchanged when not under HOME
registerInConfig writes the portable path to config.json
Duplicate detection still works: existing absolute /Users/... entry prevents re-registration of the same path as ~/...
Both / and \\ separators are handled for cross-platform compatibility

userStories.title: Unknown config key warnings with Levenshtein suggestions
userStories.description: Add validation to parseConfigRecord that warns about unrecognized top-level config keys, with Levenshtein distance suggestions for likely typos.
acceptanceCriteria: KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS set includes: version, localPlugins, localPluginDirs, permissions, settings, additionalAllowedDirectories
Unknown top-level keys in config.json produce a log.warn with the key name
When an unknown key has Levenshtein distance <= 3 from a known key, the warning includes 'did you mean X?'
Known keys do NOT produce warnings
Server starts successfully despite unknown keys (warning, not error)
Unit tests cover the Levenshtein function and the warning behavior

userStories.title: File watcher for localPluginDirs parent directories
userStories.description: Add a new watcher category in file-watcher.ts that watches localPluginDirs parent directories for new plugin subdirectories appearing, triggering re-discovery in dev mode.
acceptanceCriteria: In dev mode, each localPluginDirs directory gets a parent-level watcher
When a new subdirectory is created in a watched localPluginDirs directory, a reload is triggered
The parent-level watcher is separate from the existing dist/ file watchers
Parent-level watchers are cleaned up in stopFileWatching
Watcher handles non-existent directories gracefully (skips them)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/file-watcher.ts (add parent directory watcher category)

Context:
The file watcher system in file-watcher.ts is complex. It manages multiple watcher types: plugin dist/ watchers, config file watcher, and mtime polling fallback. The FileWatchingState (defined in file-watcher.ts or state.ts) groups these together.

startFileWatching receives resolved plugin paths and sets up watchers on each plugin's dist/ directory. The new watcher category watches at a HIGHER level — the parent directory itself (e.g., ~/workspace/src/opentabs/plugins/) for new children appearing.

Changes:
Read file-watcher.ts carefully before implementing. The existing watcher architecture uses:
- `fs.watch` on directories with debouncing
- Generation counters to safely ignore stale callbacks after hot reload
- An `onConfigChanged` callback for triggering reloads

Add a new function (e.g., `startPluginDirWatchers`) that:
1. Takes the localPluginDirs array and the onConfigChanged callback
2. For each dir, calls fs.watch on the parent directory
3. On change events, filters for directory creation (not file changes)
4. Debounces and calls onConfigChanged to trigger rediscovery
5. Stores watchers in FileWatchingState for cleanup

Integrate this into the startFileWatching function so it's called alongside the existing watchers.

Constraints:
- This watcher is dev mode ONLY (same as existing file watchers).
- Do NOT modify the existing plugin dist/ watchers.
- Do NOT trigger a full reload on every file change in the parent dir — only when the directory listing changes (new child dir appeared or was removed).
- The debounce should be longer than the dist/ watcher debounce (e.g., 1000ms) since directory creation + plugin build takes time.

Traps:
- fs.watch on macOS with FSEvents can fire multiple times for a single change. The existing code already handles this with debouncing — follow the same pattern.
- The watcher should NOT fire for changes INSIDE existing plugin directories (those are handled by the dist/ watchers). Only fire for new/removed immediate children.

userStories.title: Filtered plugin builds: --filter and --affected flags
userStories.description: Add --filter=name1,name2 and --affected flags to scripts/plugins.ts to support building a subset of plugins instead of all 113+.
acceptanceCriteria: --filter=slack,discord builds only the named plugins (exact match on directory name)
--filter with non-existent name prints available plugins and exits 1
--affected compares SDK version in each plugin's package-lock.json against current platform/plugin-sdk/package.json version
--affected skips plugins whose installed SDK version matches current (prints 'up to date')
--affected treats plugins without package-lock.json as affected
--affected when all plugins are up to date prints message and exits 0
--filter and --affected combined uses intersection (must match name AND be affected)
build:plugins:affected npm script is added to root package.json

userStories.title: E2E tests for localPluginDirs discovery and config migration
userStories.description: Write comprehensive E2E tests covering localPluginDirs directory scanning, path deduplication, the v2→v3 config migration, and portable path normalization.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: plugins in localPluginDirs directories are auto-discovered and appear in /health
Test: localPlugins entries take precedence over localPluginDirs-scanned duplicates
Test: non-existent localPluginDirs entry is silently skipped
Test: empty directory discovers zero additional plugins
Test: directories without opentabs field are skipped
Test: v2→v3 migration normalizes absolute paths to ~/
Test: migration preserves relative paths unchanged
Test: migration is idempotent
Test: server resolves ~/ paths correctly
All tests use isolated configDir and temporary plugin directories
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/local-plugin-dirs.e2e.ts (new file)
- e2e/portable-paths.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
Existing E2E test patterns to follow:
- e2e/config-migration.e2e.ts — the most relevant example. It creates isolated configDirs, writes v1 configs, starts the server, and verifies migration happened.
- e2e/tool-dispatch.e2e.ts — shows how to start the server with custom env, create minimal plugins, call /health.

Key infrastructure:
- Tests create temporary directories for configDir and plugin dirs
- Tests start the MCP server as a child process with OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR env var
- Tests use dynamic ports (port 0 or read from server output)
- Tests call /health endpoint to verify plugin discovery
- Tests read config.json after server startup to verify migration

The test for localPluginDirs should:
1. Create a temp parent dir with 2-3 minimal plugin subdirectories (package.json with opentabs field + dist/adapter.iife.js + dist/tools.json)
2. Write config.json with localPluginDirs pointing to the parent dir
3. Start the server
4. Call /health and verify all plugins are discovered

The test for path deduplication should:
1. Create a parent dir with plugin 'foo'
2. Write config with both localPlugins: ['<parent>/foo'] AND localPluginDirs: ['<parent>']
3. Start server, verify 'foo' appears exactly once in /health

For migration tests:
1. Write a v2 config with absolute paths under HOME
2. Start server (triggers migration)
3. Read config.json, verify paths are now ~/...
4. Verify version is 3

Constraints:
- Each test must be fully isolated (own configDir, own temp dirs).
- Clean up temp dirs after each test.
- Use the createMinimalPlugin helper if it exists, or create one following existing patterns.
- Tests must work in CI (no hardcoded paths, no user-specific assumptions).

userStories.title: E2E tests for reload coalescing and batch suppression
userStories.description: Write E2E tests covering reload coalescing behavior, concurrent request handling, and the OPENTABS_SKIP_NOTIFY batch suppression.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: concurrent POST /reload requests are coalesced (fire 5 simultaneously, all get 200)
Test: rapid sequential reloads within 500ms are coalesced
Test: reload after 600ms gap performs a separate reload
Test: auth is checked per-request (invalid auth returns 401 even during coalescing)
Test: POST /reload no longer returns 429
All tests use isolated configDir and dynamic ports
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/reload-coalescing.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
Follow the patterns in existing E2E tests. The tests need to:
1. Start an MCP server with a known config
2. Send multiple concurrent POST /reload requests
3. Verify all get 200 responses with correct plugin counts
4. Verify server logs show only one reload (not 5)

For concurrent requests:
```
const results = await Promise.all([
  fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/reload`, { method: 'POST', headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` } }),
  fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/reload`, { method: 'POST', headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` } }),
  // ... 3 more
]);
for (const r of results) expect(r.status).toBe(200);
```

For the 429 regression test:
- Fire 15 rapid requests
- ALL should return 200 (not 429), since rate limiting is replaced by coalescing

For auth test:
- Fire one request with valid auth and one with invalid auth simultaneously
- Valid gets 200, invalid gets 401

Constraints:
- Timing-dependent tests should use generous timeouts and retry logic.
- The coalescing window is 500ms — tests that verify coalescing should fire requests within 200ms of each other.
- Tests that verify separate reloads should wait >600ms between requests.

userStories.title: E2E tests for version check timing, unknown config keys, and filtered builds
userStories.description: Write E2E tests covering checkForUpdates removal from reload path, unknown config key warnings, and filtered plugin builds.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: POST /reload does NOT trigger npm view calls (version check absent from reload logs)
Test: unknown config key produces warning in server output
Test: close-match unknown key includes 'did you mean' suggestion
Test: server starts successfully with unknown keys and plugins are still discovered
Test: --filter=name builds only the named plugin
Test: --affected skips up-to-date plugins
All tests use isolated environments
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/version-check-timing.e2e.ts (new file)
- e2e/unknown-config-keys.e2e.ts (new file)
- e2e/plugin-build-filter.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
These are three separate test files testing independent features. Each should be a focused test suite.

For version-check-timing:
- Start server, wait for startup
- POST /reload
- Capture server logs (read from stderr/stdout)
- Verify no 'npm view' or version check activity appears after the reload
- Note: The startup version check runs async — it may appear in early logs. The test should verify it does NOT appear in logs AFTER the POST /reload timestamp.

For unknown-config-keys:
- Write config with unknown key like { localPlugins: [], permissions: {}, settings: {}, typo_field: true }
- Start server
- Capture server output
- Verify warning about 'typo_field'
- Write config with { localPlugin: [] } (close match)
- Start server
- Verify warning includes 'did you mean localPlugins'

For plugin-build-filter:
- This tests scripts/plugins.ts directly, not the MCP server
- Create a temp plugins directory with 3 minimal plugins
- Run `tsx scripts/plugins.ts --build --filter=plugin1` with modified pluginsDir
- Verify only plugin1 was built (check stdout)
- Run with --affected and a matching SDK version — verify plugin is skipped

Note: Testing --affected requires creating a minimal plugin with a package-lock.json that has a specific SDK version. This may be complex — focus on the --filter tests as the primary coverage and add --affected tests if feasible.

Constraints:
- The plugin-build-filter tests run scripts/plugins.ts as a child process, not the MCP server.
- Version check timing tests need to distinguish between startup version check (expected) and reload version check (should not happen).
- Use unique prefixes in temp directories to avoid collisions with parallel test workers.
